records.ety: [L. salivatus, p. p. of salivare to salivate. See Saliva.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Salivated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Salivating.]

defs: To produce an abnormal flow of saliva in; to produce salivation or ptyalism in, as by the use of mercury.
To produce saliva, esp. in excess.
notes: Probably influenced by the experiments of Pavlov, who trained dogs to salivate at the sound of a bell, by previously ringing the bell immediately prior to feeding them.
